# NR4A1/2/3 paralogue-differential SURFACE atlas (orientation-first RUNG-4, $0 CPU)

**Status:** design prep, not validated. Generated by
[`nr4a_differential_atlas.py`](./nr4a_differential_atlas.py) →
[`nr4a3-differential-surface-atlas.json`](./nr4a3-differential-surface-atlas.json). This is the **RUNG-4 free
CPU step** of the orientation-first prospective ladder in [STRATEGY.md](../../STRATEGY.md) — the differential
map the orientation-basin search (RUNG 5a) needs *before* any linker is designed, and the ladder's **cheap early
NO-GO gate**.

## Why this exists

The orientation-first thesis: paralogue-selective degradation is created on the **E3-facing PPI surface +
differential lysine geometry**, not the conserved warhead pocket (precedent: BRD4-vs-BRD2/3 charge zipper,
CDK4-vs-6, VAV1). So before searching E3 orientations we map, per NR4A3 residue, whether it is **(a) solvent-
exposed** (reachable by a recruited E3), **(b) divergent** vs NR4A1/NR4A2, and **(c) chemically non-conservative /
character-changing**. Their intersection = the **differential surface** = the candidate "selectivity wedges" an
E3 could be steered against.

## Method (pure stdlib — runs in the dev sandbox at $0)

- **SASA:** Shrake–Rupley (96-point Fibonacci sphere, probe 1.4 Å, spatial-grid neighbour search), normalised to
  relative SASA (RSA) by Tien et al. 2013 theoretical max-ASA; **exposed = RSA ≥ 0.25**.
- **Alignment:** Needleman–Wunsch with **affine gaps** (open −11, extend −1; BLOSUM62) of NR4A3 vs NR4A1 and vs
  NR4A2.
- **Character-change typing** on divergent positions (charge lost/gained/reversed, H-bond lost/gained, aromatic).
- **Inputs:** the matched, state-matched opened-LBD models already in the repo,
  `results/nr4a3-matrix/nr4a{3,1,2}-opened.pdb` (identical construction per paralogue → a fair matched
  comparison; NR4A3 LBD local 1..254 == UniProt 373–626).

**Validation:** the per-residue paralogue identities reproduce the canonical repo tool
(`nr4a-selectivity.json`, built independently from AFDB) on **148/148** known pocket residues (0 mismatch after
the affine-gap fix). The atlas *adds* SASA/exposure, full-LBD coverage, and character typing that the pocket-only
json lacks.

## Key result

| metric | value |
|---|---|
| residues aligned | 254 |
| exposed (RSA ≥ 0.25) | 137 |
| divergent vs ≥1 paralogue | 109 (**42.9 %**) |
| **differential-surface handles** (exposed × divergent × character-changing) | **46** |
| …of which divergent vs **both** paralogues | 33 |
| LBD lysines (all exposed) | 15 / 15 |

**GATE verdict: PASS / GO** — a differential surface exists to steer a recruited E3 against, distinct from the
buried warhead pocket. Proceed to the RUNG-5a orientation-basin search. (Had this returned no exposed divergent
handle, the ladder would stop here with an honest negative for ~$0.)

**Context:** 42.9 % LBD-wide divergence matches the selectivity-architecture doc's ~43–45 % LBD-wide figure; the
warhead pocket itself is the *hotspot* at ~70 %. So the surface is **less** selectivity-rich than the pocket but
**not conserved** — a real, independent set of handles, consistent with the multiplicative-budget thesis.

### Top differential-surface handles (by exposure)

| UniProt | NR4A3 → (NR4A1 / NR4A2) | RSA | vs both | character change |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| U576 | L → (G / N) | 0.95 | ✔ | hbond gained (vs NR4A2) |
| U574 | Q → (V / G) | 0.88 | ✔ | hbond lost (both) |
| U412 | R → (A / T) | 0.78 | ✔ | **charge lost (both)** |
| U424 | Q → (L / Y) | 0.66 | ✔ | hbond lost / aromatic |
| U413 | D → (K / S) | 0.61 | ✔ | **charge reversed (vs NR4A1)** |
| U614 | S → (P / A) | 0.63 | ✔ | hbond lost (both) |
| U396 | I → (A / V) | 0.68 | ✔ | steric (both) |

(U412/U413 sit at the pocket rim — the R412 handle already noted in the design spec; U574/U576/U614 are genuine
surface patches away from the pocket. Full ranked list in the JSON.)

## Honest limits (carried from the JSON)

- **Single static opened conformer per paralogue** — not an MD ensemble. The matched NR4A1/2 MD ensembles are the
  optional ~$10–40 Vast-3090 add-on; a handle that survives the ensemble is stronger than one seen in one frame.
- **LBD-only.** Hinge / DBD / **EWSR1::NR4A3 fusion** lysines are **not** in these models — the 15 LBD lysines are
  not necessarily the only (or best) degradable sites; a full-length/fusion model is required for that.
- **RSA is a solvent-exposure proxy for "E3-reachable,"** not a docked-interface calculation — that is exactly
  what the RUNG-5a orientation-basin search does next. A handle here is a *hypothesis* to steer an E3 against, and
  its causal role is only established later by the reciprocal mutation-cycle kill-switch (RUNG 5a-KS).
- Design prep, conditional on the opened-state models chosen; not validated.

## How it feeds the ladder

1. **RUNG 5a orientation-basin search** samples E3 placements and asks which basins contact these differential-
   surface handles on NR4A3 but not on NR4A1/2.
2. **RUNG 5a-KS kill-switch** picks the highest-exposure divergent handles here (e.g. U412, U413, U574) as the
   first reciprocal-mutation-cycle targets — the causal test of whether a handle actually drives cooperativity.
